commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining action is maintainer/security review of the dependency rollback plus full CI, not an automated repair.; Needs attention: The patch is coherent, but it intentionally downgrades a security-sensitive filesystem dependency to restore POSIX Node fallback writes that the newer version fails closed. (sha=1c004575255045b86b5cfd855d76b019a9e2998e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR pins `@openclaw/fs-safe` to `0.2.5`, updates related pi and Bedrock guardrails, removes an obsolete Windows ACL test, adds a changelog entry, and adjusts OpenRouter spread fallbacks for oxlint.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main sets fs-safe Python mode to `off`, and upstream `@openclaw/fs-safe` `0.2.6` throws on non-Windows pinned writes in that mode. I did not run tests because this review is read-only, but the package source and PR test report make the failure path high-confidence.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer/security review of the dependency rollback plus full CI, not an automated repair.

target.title: fix: revert @openclaw/fs-safe to 0.2.5 to restore pinned-write fallback
target.body:
## Summary

CI has been red on `main` since 94ac563399 (`build: update dependencies`). The dep bump took `@openclaw/fs-safe` from 0.2.4 to 0.2.6, and 0.2.6 changed `runPinnedWriteHelper` so the `configureFsSafePython({ mode: "off" })` path now calls `runPinnedWriteFallbackOrThrow`, which throws on every non-Windows platform:

```
FsSafeError: Python helper is required for pinned writes on this platform
  at runPinnedWriteFallbackOrThrow node_modules/@openclaw/fs-safe/dist/pinned-write.js:131
```

`src/infra/fs-safe-defaults.ts` sets that off mode by default, so every test that touches a private file store, secret-file write, cron run log, audio transcode, or managed image attachment fails on Linux/macOS. That is the bulk of the red CI lanes (`checks-node-core-src-security`, `checks-node-core-runtime-{cron,media-ui,infra-state}`, `checks-node-agentic-{plugin-sdk,cli,gateway-core,gateway-methods,control-plane-agent-chat}`, etc.).

Three other failures are aftershocks of the same dep bump:

- `src/security/windows-acl.test.ts > classifies principal with diacritic not in TRUSTED_BASE but matching stripped suffix (line 145)` — 0.2.5 already removed the `TRUSTED_SUFFIXES` suffix-matching branch as a security fix (broad-domain ACL principals like `EVIL\Système` must no longer be trusted). The test was pinned to the old behavior.
- `test/scripts/root-package-overrides.test.ts > keeps Bedrock runtime ownership in the Amazon provider plugin` — pnpm override bumped to `3.1048.0`, guardrail still asserted `3.1045.0`.
- `src/scripts/control-ui-i18n.test.ts > keeps the fallback pi package version aligned with the workspace dependency` — `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` bumped to `0.75.3`, fallback constant still `0.75.1`.

## Changes

- `package.json`: pin `@openclaw/fs-safe` to `0.2.5` (one step back from 0.2.6, keeps the 0.2.5 security tightening, restores Node fallback for off mode on non-Windows).
- `scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts`: bump `DEFAULT_PI_PACKAGE_VERSION` to `0.75.3`.
- `test/scripts/root-package-overrides.test.ts`: expect Bedrock override `3.1048.0`.
- `src/security/windows-acl.test.ts`: drop the test that exercised the removed `TRUSTED_SUFFIXES` branch. The surrounding French/German/Spanish SYSTEM coverage stays.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Unreleased fixes entry.

Once `@openclaw/fs-safe` ships a fix that lets `mode: "off"` fall back to Node on Linux/macOS again (or we decide to require Python and remove the off default), we can bump back to 0.2.6+.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/security/windows-acl.test.ts src/cron/run-log.test.ts test/scripts/root-package-overrides.test.ts src/scripts/control-ui-i18n.test.ts` → 92/92 pass.
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commitments/ src/cron/run-log.test.ts src/media/audio-transcode.test.ts src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/fs-safe-compat.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts src/ui-app-settings.agents-files-refresh.test.ts` → 252/252 pass (covers every test file CI flagged on the latest red `main` run).
- [ ] Full CI on PR.

### Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: previously failing CI lanes after 94ac563399 (`fs-safe` off-mode pinned-write regression, stale Bedrock/pi version guardrails, removed `TRUSTED_SUFFIXES` security branch).
- Real environment tested: local macOS worktree (`darwin-arm64`, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install`, then the two `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` invocations above.
- Evidence after fix: `Test Files 4 passed (4)` / `Tests 92 passed (92)` and `Test Files 15 passed (15)` / `Tests 252 passed (252)`.
- Observed result after fix: every previously failing test file is green locally.
- What was not tested: full `pnpm check`/CI lanes (deferred to PR CI), Windows behavior (no upstream regression there since 0.2.6 still allows the Node fallback on `win32`).
target.author: romneyda
